title: system tests: add assert(), and start using it
body:
Problem: the system test 'is()' checker was poorly thought out.
For example, there is no way to check for inequality or for
absence of a substring.

Solution, step 1: introduce new assert(), copied almost verbatim
from buildah, where it has been successful in addressing the
gaps in is().

The logical next step is to search the tests for 'die' and
for 'run', looking for negative assertions which we can
replace with assert(). There were a lot, and in the process
I found a number of ugly bugs in the tests themselves. I've
taken the liberty of fixing these.

Important note: at this time we have both assert() and is().
Replacing all instances of is() would be impossible to review.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
/hold

DO NOT MERGE YET. I've rebased on top of #13936 to test that the skip-e2e-tests logic works.

LGTM but we should fix https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13935#discussion_r854443035 soon. This is a very bad idea.

>LGTM but we should fix https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13935#discussion_r854443035 soon. This is a very bad idea

@Luap99 is the current implementation (running the test only in `$container` environment) not a good enough balance between mount namespace (hard) and risk (who cares what happens to a containerized test environment)?

The current $container check never runs the test because there are no systems which run in a container AFAICT.
I am fine with the check but we have to make sure that it will actually run, otherwise we should remove the test.
